result:
**Phase 1 complete.** 

**What changed:** Replaced `Peezy 4.0/Assessment/AssessmentViews/Components/TypingText.swift` with the AttributedString-based approach. The old implementation built a new `String(fullText.prefix(n))` on every tick, causing SwiftUI to recompute text layout each frame (jitter with centered/multi-line text). The new implementation always renders the full text via `Text(attributedText)` and only toggles character colors from `.clear` to visible — the layout is computed once and never changes. No other files were modified. Build succeeds.
